sn1.10:1.0.1: At Sāvatthī. Standing to one side, that devata recited this verse in the presence of the Blessed One:
sn1.10:1.0.2: v.17"Those who dwell deep in the forest,Peaceful, leading the holy life,Eating but a single meal a day:Why is their complexion so serene?"
sn1.10:1.0.3: v.18"They do not sorrow over the past,Nor do they hanker for the future.They maintain themselves with what is present:Hence their complexion is so serene.
sn1.10:1.0.4: v.19"Through hankering for the future,Through sorrowing over the past,Fools dry up and wither awayLike a green reed cut down."
